response:
To find the **greatest common divisor (GCD)** of the set $\{-1403, 751\}$, we first note that the GCD of a set of integers is the **largest positive integer** that divides all of them without leaving a remainder.

---

### Step 1: Use the Euclidean Algorithm

We apply the Euclidean Algorithm to the two numbers:

$$
\gcd(-1403, 751)
$$

Since the GCD is unaffected by the sign of the numbers, we can work with the positive values:

$$
\gcd(1403, 751)
$$

---

### Step 2: Apply the Euclidean Algorithm

We repeatedly apply the algorithm:

$$
1403 \div 751 = 1 \text{ remainder } 652
$$

Now compute:

$$
\gcd(751, 652)
$$

$$
751 \div 652 = 1 \text{ remainder } 99
$$

Now compute:

$$
\gcd(652, 99)
$$

$$
652 \div 99 = 6 \text{ remainder } 68
$$

Now compute:

$$
\gcd(99, 68)
$$

$$
99 \div 68 = 1 \text{ remainder } 31
$$

Now compute:

$$
\gcd(68, 31)
$$

$$
68 \div 31 = 2 \text{ remainder } 6
$$

Now compute:

$$
\gcd(31, 6)
$$

$$
31 \div 6 = 5 \text{ remainder } 1
$$

Now compute:

$$
\gcd(6, 1)
$$

$$
6 \div 1 = 6 \text{ remainder } 0
$$

So the GCD is:

$$
\gcd(1403, 751) = 1
$$

---

### Final Answer:

$$
\boxed{1}
$$
